text: most vibe coders ship apps with zero security. no rate limiting. no RLS. API keys in the client bundle. then they wonder why their Supabase bill hit $500 from endpoint abuse. I built a 47-point checklist that takes 30 minutes to run through. stop shipping vulnerable apps.

just ran a security scan on 20 vibe-coded apps from twitter. 17 out of 20 had their Supabase anon key confused with the service role key. that means every visitor has admin access to the entire database. takes 2 minutes to fix. most won't.

your Cursor-generated app is not secure by default. Cursor writes functional code not secure code. it will happily put your OpenAI key in a client component because you asked for 'an AI chat feature.' grep your build folder for sk- right now. I'll wait.

the #1 security mistake I see in vibe-coded apps: no rate limiting on AI endpoints. your feature costs $0.01 per request. someone scripts 100K requests. thats $1,000 on your credit card overnight. 5 lines of Upstash code fixes this. 5 lines.

'I'll add security later' - every indie hacker who then gets their user database scraped at 10 users. security at 10 users = 30 minutes. security after a breach = weeks of damage control + lost trust + potential legal issues. pick one.

quick security test for your Next.js app: open devtools. go to network tab. look at your API calls. can you see API keys in the request headers? if yes, anyone visiting your site can see them too. move those calls server-side. today.

built an automated security audit script. point it at any URL and it checks: security headers, CORS config, API key exposure, sensitive file leaks, SSL issues, Supabase/Firebase misconfig. runs in 30 seconds. found critical issues in 8 out of 10 apps I tested.

client-side authorization is not security. if (user.role === 'admin') in your React component means anyone can open DevTools, change the role to admin, and see everything. ALWAYS check roles server-side. this is the most common vibe coder mistake and it takes 5 minutes to fix.

shipped a security checklist for vibe coders. 47 checks organized by severity. copy-paste code snippets for every fix. covers auth, RLS, API keys, rate limiting, CORS, headers, dependencies. designed to complete in 30 minutes. because your app deserves more than 'it works.'

you deployed your app to Vercel and think you're secure. Vercel handles deployment security. they don't handle YOUR app security. RLS, rate limiting, input validation, API key management = all your responsibility. Vercel keeps the house standing. you still need to lock the doors.
